name: FUMANA
description: Dual all-analog 16-band spectral filter bank. Parallel bandpass array with per-band faders and CV, Tilt/Scan macros, odd/even splits, and a dual envelope-follower modulator path for 16-band vocoding and dynamic spectral transfer.

controls.name: Band Faders (×16)
controls.description: Per-band amplitude of the main 16 bandpass filters.
controls.detail: bottom: VCA closed · top: unity · red LED per band

controls.name: Main Odd Level
controls.description: Input gain for odd-band main signal.

controls.name: Main Even Level
controls.description: Input gain for even-band main signal.

controls.name: Mod Odd Level
controls.description: Input gain for odd-band modulator signal (vocoder input).

controls.name: Mod Even Level
controls.description: Input gain for even-band modulator signal.

controls.name: Unvoiced Level
controls.description: Input gain for the fricative/sibilant detector channel.
controls.detail: white/pink noise recommended · detects sharp transients in bands 14 & 15 of modulator

controls.name: Tilt
controls.description: Seesaw between low and high bands around fulcrum at bands 8/9.
controls.detail: CCW: emphasise 1-8 · noon: flat · CW: emphasise 9-16 · ±10 V CV

controls.name: Peak/Notch
controls.description: Positive gain (peak) or negative gain (notch) at the Scan centre.
controls.detail: CCW: notch · noon: no emphasis · CW: peak · ±10 V CV input

controls.name: Center
controls.description: Centre frequency of the parametric scan.
controls.detail: CCW: band 1 · CW: band 16 · continuous · ±10 V CV

controls.name: Width
controls.description: Number of bands flanking Centre affected by Peak/Notch.
controls.detail: CCW: only centre band · CW: all 16 bands · Centre audible only if Width > 0 · ±10 V CV

controls.name: E.F. Attack
controls.description: Attack time of the 16 modulator envelope followers.
controls.detail: CCW: fastest transient response · non-conventional (for formant sculpt)

controls.name: E.F. Release
controls.description: Release time of the 16 modulator envelope followers.

controls.name: CV Input Attenuverters
controls.description: Scales the CV input to each macro control.

controls.name: Odd/Even Phase Inversion Switch
controls.description: Inverts phase of Odd or Even sum relative to All.
controls.detail: for dynamic spectral subtraction / reinforcement when summed externally

inputs.name: Main Odd In / Main Even In
inputs.description: Carrier audio input, pre-split into odd and even bands. Semi-normalled together so one cable feeds both.

inputs.name: Mod Odd In / Mod Even In
inputs.description: Modulator audio (vocoder analysis source). Fed through 16 analysis bandpass filters + envelope followers.
inputs.normalizedTo: odd ↔ even when only one patched

inputs.name: Unvoiced In
inputs.description: Additional input for fricatives/sibilants. Detector reroutes bands 14 & 15 to this audio when detected.

inputs.name: Individual Band CV Inputs (×16)
inputs.description: Per-band VCA CV, one under each fader. Breaks semi-normal from envelope follower.
inputs.normalizedTo: corresponding envelope follower output (sum at input)

inputs.name: Peak/Notch CV
inputs.description: Voltage over the parametric Gain macro.

inputs.name: Center CV
inputs.description: Voltage over the parametric Center frequency.

outputs.name: All Out
outputs.description: Sum of all 16 filtered bands with additional 8th-order lowpass at 18 kHz. Slightly smoother than summing Odd+Even.

outputs.name: Odd Out
outputs.description: Sum of bands 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15 post-VCA. Pre-VCA individual filter taps not present here.

outputs.name: Individual Band Outputs (×16)
outputs.description: Pre-VCA direct filter tap per band. Not affected by the fader or VCA — parallel-process a band independently.

outputs.name: All E.F. Out
outputs.description: Sum of all 16 envelope followers. Composite amplitude envelope of the modulator signal.

outputs.name: Individual E.F. Outputs (×16)
outputs.description: Per-band envelope-follower CV. Usable as 16-band spectral analysis signal independent of the VCAs.

behaviors.name: 16-band spectral transfer
behaviors.trigger: patch carrier → Main in, modulator → Mod in
behaviors.description: Modulator feeds its own 16-filter analysis array. Each analysis filter drives an envelope follower whose output is semi-normalled to the corresponding main-filter VCA CV. The modulator's spectral footprint opens the carrier's matching bands. Classic 16-band analog vocoding when carrier is harmonic.

behaviors.name: Sixth-order modulator filters
behaviors.description: Mod analysis filters are 6th-order (36 dB/oct) with a further stage that compensates band energy — distinct from the main 8th-order array (48 dB/oct). Band extraction is slightly less steep on the analysis side by design for smoother envelope curves.

behaviors.name: Semi-normal per-band break
behaviors.trigger: patch a cable into any Individual Band CV input
behaviors.description: Envelope-follower normal to each VCA is broken only for that specific band when a jack is patched. The other 15 bands keep the envelope-following behaviour. Patch Individual E.F. outs in a shuffled order to bend-vocode (voice hits at one frequency open a different band on the carrier).

behaviors.name: Odd/Even splitting
behaviors.trigger: patch only Odd or Even input
behaviors.description: Inputs are semi-normalled in pairs (Main Odd ↔ Main Even, Mod Odd ↔ Mod Even). When one jack is patched it auto-feeds the other. Break the normal by patching both to different sources: the module becomes two 8-band vocoders operating independently on odd and even bands.

behaviors.name: Parametric scanning
behaviors.description: Acts as a three-variable parametric EQ across the 16 bands. Centre selects fulcrum band, Width extends emphasis to neighbours, Peak/Notch sets positive (peak) or negative (notch) gain. Centre is audible only when Width > fully-CCW. Off position for Peak/Notch sits around noon but varies per unit.

behaviors.name: Tilt fulcrum at 8/9
behaviors.description: Tilt pivots around bands 8-9. Emphasises one half while attenuating the other proportionally — more distant bands are affected more strongly. Use a kick-drum envelope into Tilt CV input to ducking-sidechain the lower spectrum on each hit, reinforcing the higher register.

behaviors.name: Unvoiced section
behaviors.trigger: patch audio into Unvoiced In + modulator into Mod In
behaviors.description: Bands 14 & 15 have dedicated second envelope followers + VCAs that switch their source from the main signal to the Unvoiced audio when the modulator hits those bands. Fricatives/sibilants in voice modulator inject noise bursts from the Unvoiced input into the carrier. Preserves sound instead of muting it (unlike a traditional de-esser).

behaviors.name: Individual band taps pre-VCA
behaviors.description: Each of the 16 direct outputs taps the filter before the fader/VCA. Patching does not affect the fader's role in the All/Odd/Even mix — use these to parallel-process a band (band 10 to a delay send, band 4 to a distortion) while the bank continues normal duty.

behaviors.name: Audio-rate CV at per-band inputs
behaviors.trigger: audio into Individual Band CV input
behaviors.description: CV inputs accept signals up to ~1 kHz with a lowpass roll-off past that. Patching an audio-rate signal into a band CV performs amplitude modulation on that single band — 16 parallel AM channels selectable per-band.

behaviors.name: Envelope follower as spectral CV source
behaviors.description: The 16 Individual E.F. outputs are usable as a 16-voice spectral analyser regardless of the main path. Take any audio through Mod In and use the envelope outs to drive external filters, VCAs, or quantised sequencers — FUMANA becomes a pure analysis tool.

patchIdeas.name: Classic 16-band vocoder
patchIdeas.patch: Square-wave VCO into Main In (blue) as carrier · voice microphone into Mod In (grey) · all faders to unity · E.F. Attack fast, E.F. Release medium · All Out delivers the sung carrier · tune the VCO to match voice fundamental.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* Faders 1-16: unity
* E.F. Attack: fast
* E.F. Release: medium
* Unvoiced In: pink noise

- VCO (Square) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- Mic Preamp (Out) -> FUMANA (Mod Odd In)
- Noise (Pink) -> FUMANA (Unvoiced In)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Dual 8-band vocoder
patchIdeas.patch: Square into Main Odd · triangle into Main Even · drums into Mod Odd · vocal into Mod Even · Odd Out = drum-modulated square (8 bands), Even Out = voice-modulated triangle (8 bands) · two independent vocoders sharing the same 16-band hardware.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* Faders 1-16: unity

- VCO 1 (Square) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- VCO 2 (Triangle) -> FUMANA (Main Even In)
- Drums (Out) -> FUMANA (Mod Odd In)
- Voice (Out) -> FUMANA (Mod Even In)
- FUMANA (Odd Out) -> Mixer (In 1)
- FUMANA (Even Out) -> Mixer (In 2)

patchIdeas.name: Spectral sidechain ducking
patchIdeas.patch: Drum bus through Main · bassline envelope into Tilt CV · each kick tilts the band distribution upward (attenuates lows) · bass breathes back in during rests · more musical than a broadband sidechain.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* Tilt CV Attenuverter: ~2 o'clock

- Drum Bus (Out) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- Kick Envelope (Out) >> FUMANA (Tilt CV)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Parametric sweep
patchIdeas.patch: Pink noise into Main · LFO into Center CV · Width set to 3 bands · Peak/Notch CW for peak · LFO sweeps a resonant peak across the 16-band spectrum · sample-and-hold at Center CV gives stepped band jumps instead of sweeps.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* Faders 1-16: unity
* Peak/Notch: CW (peak)
* Width: ~3 bands

- Noise (Pink) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- LFO (Out) >> FUMANA (Center CV)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Cross-wired vocoder
patchIdeas.patch: Voice into Mod In · VCO into Main · patch Individual E.F. outputs into Individual Band CV inputs in a shuffled pattern — E.F. 1 → Band 16 CV, E.F. 16 → Band 1 · high voice formants open low carrier bands and vice versa · inverted spectral character.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:

- Voice (Out) -> FUMANA (Mod Odd In)
- VCO (Saw) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- FUMANA (E.F. 1 Out) >> FUMANA (Band 16 CV)
- FUMANA (E.F. 16 Out) >> FUMANA (Band 1 CV)
- FUMANA (E.F. 8 Out) >> FUMANA (Band 9 CV)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: 16-band analyser
patchIdeas.patch: Audio into Mod Odd · take the 16 Individual E.F. outputs as spectral CVs · route the All E.F. out as a broadband amplitude envelope · use spectral CVs to drive external oscillators for additive resynthesis.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:

- Audio Src (Out) -> FUMANA (Mod Odd In)
- FUMANA (E.F. 1 Out) >> VCO 1 (Amp CV)
- FUMANA (E.F. 4 Out) >> VCO 2 (Amp CV)
- FUMANA (E.F. 8 Out) >> VCO 3 (Amp CV)
- FUMANA (All E.F. Out) >> Master VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Per-band effects send
patchIdeas.patch: Individual Band 10 Out → delay send · delay return mixed back in · the tenth band of the spectrum gets an echo tail while all other bands stay dry · carrier bank continues to pass full signal via All Out · parallel wet band.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* Faders 1-16: unity

- Carrier (Out) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- FUMANA (Band 10 Out) -> FX (In)
- FX (Out) -> Mixer (Wet In)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Mixer (Dry In)

patchIdeas.name: Spectral freeze with faders
patchIdeas.patch: Patch modulator with brief phrase · set E.F. Release CW (long) · immediately after the phrase, all VCAs stay open at their last-sampled levels · manual fader adjustments freeze a static spectral emphasis derived from the sampled moment.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* E.F. Attack: fast
* E.F. Release: very long (CW)

- Voice (Out) -> FUMANA (Mod Odd In)
- Drone (Out) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Notch scan for hollowness
patchIdeas.patch: Voice through Main · Peak/Notch CCW (notch) · Width ~half · slow LFO into Center CV · a 3-band notch crawls across the voice spectrum creating cyclic tonal absence · strong phasing-like effect without phaser delay.
patchIdeas.diagram:
FUMANA:
* Peak/Notch: CCW (notch)
* Width: ~half

- Voice (Out) -> FUMANA (Main Odd In)
- Slow LFO (Out) >> FUMANA (Center CV)
- FUMANA (All Out) -> Output (In)
